sourceMovie.title: Get Smart
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Get Smart (2008) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: RED
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Get Smart clicked for you, RED (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Mission: Impossible
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Get Smart clicked for you, Mission: Impossible (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Get Smart?
faq.answer: Try RED, Mission: Impossible, The Man from U.N.C.L.E.—each shares DNA with Get Smart while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Get Smart?
faq.answer: Get Smart (2008) is best described as Action, Comedy, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Get Smart worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
